entry.title: Re: Let's code association/navigation and data provider expand in OData service!

entry.title: Re: Error Creating Content Provider for SAP Build Work Zo...
entry.content:
Hi,

I faced the same issue when integrating a multi-tenant SAPUI5 app into SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition. Here’s what I discovered. The problem often lies in the CDM Runtime destination URL, which usually looks like this:

https://html5-apps-repo-rt.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/applications/cdm/<business_solution>

There are two key points that caused the internal error in my case:
1. Make sure the <region> in the URL is just the region code, e.g.: eu10, us20. Do not use eu10-004, even if that’s how your subdomain appears in the runtime URL.

2. The value you use for <business_solution> (i.e. the sap.cloud.service in your manifest.json) must be globally unique across the region. If another team (or even yourself in the past) has already deployed an app using the same sap.cloud.service, then attempting to register it again from another tenant will cause:

<Connectivity/InternalError>: Could not fetch content due to an internal error...

And in Postman you may see:

Error while getting CDM for tenant <tenant-id> and sapCloudService <business_solution> { CODE: '5081' }

This also sometimes happens if you undeploy and redeploy an app using the same service name.

Solution that worked for me: I changed the value of sap.cloud.service to a new, unique name in both: manifest.json and mta.yaml (under the destination URL). After redeploying with this new sap.cloud.service name, Work Zone was able to successfully register the content provider.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: How to Connect Eclipse with Git or abapGit and Push A...
entry.content:
Hi @Ayyaparaja ,

Yes, you can absolutely push ABAP code directly from Eclipse ADT to a GitHub repository. Based on the plugins you've installed, the correct tool for this is the abapGit plugin for ADT.

Here is a comprehensive guide to help you link your repository and manage your code.

Prerequisites in GitHub
Before you begin in ADT, please complete the following steps in GitHub:

  1. Create a New Repository: If you haven't already, create a new repository. It is recommended to initialize it with a README.md file. This ensures that a default branch (e.g., main or master) is created, which is required for the initial linking process in ADT.
  2. Copy the Repository URL: Copy the HTTPS URL of your repository. You will need this to link it within ADT.
  3. Generate a Personal Access Token (PAT): If you are connecting to a private repository on github.com or an internal company instance (like github.tools.sap), you must generate a Personal Access Token. This token will be used instead of your password for authentication from ADT. Ensure the token has the necessary scopes (e.g., repo) to allow read and write access to the repository.


Linking Your Repository in ADT
Follow these steps to link your local ABAP package to the remote Git repository:

  1. Open the abapGit Repositories View: In ADT, use the shortcut Ctrl+3 (for Windows) or Cmd+3 (for macOS) and type abapGit Repositories to open the view. This view lists all the repository links set up in your ABAP system.
  2. Initiate a New Repository Link: In the top-right corner of the "abapGit Repositories" view, click the +
  3.  (Add) icon. This will start the process to link a new remote repository to an ABAP package

Please Note: This "linking" step does not clone or pull any code. It simply establishes a persistent connection between a specific ABAP package and a specific branch of your Git repository. The current architecture permits a one-to-one relationship; one package can be linked to only one repository branch at a time.


Complete the "Link Repository" Wizard:

  1. Repository URL: On the first page of the wizard, paste the repository URL you copied from GitHub.
  2. Authentication: If the repository is private or on an internal enterprise server, you will be prompted for credentials. Enter your GitHub username and use the Personal Access Token (PAT) you generated as your password.
  3. Branch and Package Selection: Choose the specific repository Branch you want to work with and select the ABAP Package you wish to link it to. All Git operations, such as pulling and pushing, will be performed between this selected pair.
  4. Initial Pull Option: If you are linking a repository for the first time to an empty package, it is recommended to uncheck the "Pull after link" option to maintain clear separation of steps. You can perform a pull manually later.
  5. Folder Logic Selection: The next page defines the "Folder Logic." This crucial setting determines the folder structure of your ABAP objects within the Git repository.
    FULL: Creates a folder structure that mirrors the ABAP object hierarchy (e.g., /src/zcl_my_class.clas.abap). This is a common and intuitive choice.
    PREFIX: Uses the package name as a top-level folder for all objects.
    NAMESPACE: Uses the SAP namespace (e.g., /0CUST/) as the top-level folder.
    Choose the logic that best suits your project's standards. For most use cases, FULL is recommended.
  6. Finalize the Link: After selecting the folder logic, click Finish. Your ABAP package and Git repository branch are now successfully linked.

 

Performing Git Operations
Once the link is established, you can perform standard Git operations from the context menu of the linked repository.

For Pulling Changes from the Repository
If the remote repository contains objects that are not yet in your ABAP system, you can pull them.

  1. Right-click on the linked repository in the "abapGit Repositories" view and select "Pull".
  2. The wizard will connect to the remote repository and show you a list of ABAP objects that are available to be imported.
  3. Select the objects you wish to import into your package.
  4. Click Continue and then Finish to complete the pull process. The selected objects will be created or updated in your ABAP system.


For Pushing Your Changes to the Repository

  1. After making changes to ABAP objects within your linked package, right-click on the repository link and choose the Stage and Push option.
  2. This action will open the "abapGit Staging" view. This view displays all modified, created, or deleted objects (both code and non-code files) in your local package that differ from the current state of the repository branch.
  3. Select the changes you wish to commit. You can add individual files or all changes to the "Staged Changes" area by using the + icon.
  4. In the "Commit Message" box, write a clear and concise message describing the changes you are pushing.
  5. Once you have staged your desired files and written a commit message, click the Commit and Push button to transfer your changes to the remote GitHub repository.

Please let me know if there is any other information I can help you with.
Thanks,
Aarnav

entry.title: Re: Where to set the tax-excluded/tax-included accounting...
entry.content:
Hello @yahaha 

Great question ,here’s a clear and precise answer, backed by SAP documentation, specific to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

Where is the configuration setting located?

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, tax calculation behaviour (inclusive vs. exclusive) is controlled through predefined SSCUIs (self-service configuration UIs), not classic SPRO paths. The key settings are:

  • SSCUI 101016 – Define Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases
    → This is where you set the Tax Code, Tax Percentage, and determine if the tax is calculated as inclusive or exclusive.
  • SSCUI 100058 – Specify Structure for Tax Calculation
    → This SSCUI determines how the system structures the base amount and calculates tax — using SAP-delivered procedures.

Official SAP Help:

Time-Dependent Taxes (Two-System Landscape

 Is Tax-Exclusive the Standard Method?

 Yes tax-exclusive accounting is the default standard in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This means:

Posting Example (Tax-Exclusive)

  • Dr. Expense (Net)
  • Dr. Input Tax (deductible VAT/GST)
  • Cr. Accounts Payable (Gross)

This is aligned with:

  • Global practices for tax reporting and input VAT reclaim
  • SAP Best Practices like scope item 2TX (Sales Tax Processing) and 1J2 (Purchasing with Tax)

See:
SAP Best Practices Explorer – Scope Item 1J2
SAP Best Practices Explorer – Scope Item 2TX

If a tax-inclusive scenario is required (e.g., full amount booked to expense without splitting tax), this must be handled via:

  • A dedicated tax code marked as “inclusive”
  • Typically used for non-deductible tax scenarios (where VAT cannot be reclaimed)

Note: S/4HANA Public Cloud does not support user-defined pricing procedures or modifications to the tax calculation schema — everything must comply with SAP-delivered configuration.

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: How to add public dimension into SAP Analytics Cloud Analytic Import Model?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm trying to use a public dimension in an import analytic model. Both the master data for the public dimension and the fact data are coming from separate ABAP CDS views exposed as OData services.

These are the steps I'm following:

1) Create the public dimension and load master data into it (see images 1 and 2). The member ID is saknr from the skat table. The description is a custom category group that is required to be added for each account number record in the fact data.

image 1

image 2

2) Create an import analytic model via the "new empty model" option. The model includes the hsl (acdoca) measure, the racct (acdoca) private dimension, and the "groups" public dimension (see image 3).

image 3

3) Create an import job for the transactional data. During the mapping, it is required to map a field from the transactional data to the public dimension (see image 4). As a test, I set the default value to #.

image 4

4) After the import is complete, the "groups" column just displays the # for all the records. The expected result is that each racct record would get its corresponding category group (see image 5).

image 5

Even in the learning journey “Designing Data Models and Transforming Data in SAP Analytics Cloud” the public dimension is mapped (see image 6 from this exercise: https://learnsap.enable-now.cloud.sap/pub/mmcp/index.html?show=project!PR_6EA6A81D112ED9AC:uebung)

image 6

In my understanding, SAC would make an automatic mapping between the id field of the public dimension and the matching field of the fact data. What am I missing here?

Thanks a lot in advance!

P.S. 1: One alternative option is to combine the data sources (OData services) of the fact data and the public dimension in the modeler, but I want to understand why the public dimension isn't working for me.

P.S. 2: I've made multiple tests and followed these resources with no success:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/mapping-public-dimension-in-model-that-imports-from-sap-bw/qaq-p/12309535

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/public-dimension-in-sac/qaq-p/12406604

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/adding-a-public-dimension-to-an-existing-model/qaq-p/12751542

https://youtu.be/X7--25qTbB0

https://youtu.be/WbT-XQOH7oo

entry.title: #CoE#GDTD导出软件集合导出软件时;提示导出失败
entry.content:
我创建了一个399类型的类，

然后通过app‘分类/配置CDS视图’生成了399类型的CDS视图，

我尝试修改并且新增这个CDS视图类下的特征，

再去生成CDS1，结果发现CDS1没有改变，

猜测可能是399类型的类只能新增不能修改，于是我新建了一个399类，然后创建了新的399CDS2。399类型CDS1作为了数据源创建了外部API类型的CDS将销售报价单中的详细特征传输出SAP，所以我尝试用CDS2替换CDS1，在导出软件时，出现报错，如图

The learning journey website for the course(Acquiring Core ABAP Skills) also indicates that it is not connected to hands-on practice.

entry.title: Re: Annotation @AbapCatalog.extensibility.allowNewDatasou...
entry.content:
seems to be a miss from sap

here you only expose it as association, you didn't select any field(from the new source), it passed sap check(tricked sap)

extend view entity /DMO/E_Agency 
       with association to zzextenagent_swa as _zzswas 
    on $projection.AgencyId = _zzswas.agencyid       
{     
     _zzswas      
}

 

 here your selection is from _Extension, which is a legitimate datasource, that's how it passed the check. I guess datasource check is only performed at current cds view, just like how the dcl is checked.

extend view entity /DMO/R_AgencyTP with {
    _Extension._zzswas.zagswascity as ZZSWASCITYZAG
}

entry.title: Re: Zero Quantity, Negative Stock Value in Inventory Audit Report

entry.title: Re: How to add public dimension into SAP Analytics Cloud...
entry.content:
It's expected that '#' is used for all rows of data you imported as you explicitly 'default to #' in step 3). 

If you don't have this column in your source data, how would you expect SAC to populate different values to this column during import?

entry.title: SSO Live Connection from SAC to HANA using CIS getting error
entry.content:
Hi expert,

I tried to create a Live Connection from SAP Analytics Cloud to SAP HANA using Connection Type Direct ( I use this Type because I want to use Smart Insight Feature of SAC) and Authentication Method SAML Single Sign On (SSO). I use SAP Cloud Identity Service (CIS) as an IdP. I followed all the instructions but still can not create a Connection. I enabled the SAML in SAP HANA Studio and also mapped the External Identity (see the images below):

Then I went to SAC to create a connection:

When I hit the OK button, a pop-up appeared and redirected to the IdP log in link and I get the error:

Then I checked the Chrome Developers Tools and get the logs:

I already added the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" in Allowed Header and Exposed Header in sap.bc.ina.service.v2 in XS Admin Tool. 
Which configs am I missing? 
Thank you experts,

Regards,

Thanh Hieu

entry.title: Filed" scrap "in routing masterdata does not exist in the Import template
entry.content: Filed" scrap "in routing masterdata does not exist in the Import template,so how should I migrate the data?There is no way to update the data after migration, so could you please tell me a good way to do this?

entry.title: Re: IBP Calculation with kf which is stored and calculate
entry.content:
Hi Olli76,

ZZAOPQTYSALESORG@MTHSALESORKPRODLOCCUST which is output of your equation. which means you have a saved value in the output of the equation (calculated). So, using the above key figure in KF3 means you are using a saved value and if there is no saved value then calculated value is used as input.

Thank you,

Lokesh Reddy

entry.title: How to Integrate SAP Ariba with Third-Party Applications – Standard APIs
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a requirement to integrate SAP Ariba (Buyer module) with third-party applications (non-SAP). I would like to know:

  • Are there any standard APIs provided by SAP Ariba for such integrations?

  • If yes, could you please share the list of those APIs or relevant documentation?

  • Is there any recommended approach or best practice for integrating Ariba with external systems?

I’m particularly looking for direct integration options (not via SAP S/4HANA or middleware) if available.

Any guidance or reference to API documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!
Best regards,
Satheesh Kumar.

entry.title: Re: SAP Group Reporting - Post manual Journal Entry on Re...
entry.content: Thank you for response.

entry.title: How to Integrate SAP IBP with Third-Party Systems – Standard APIs
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a business requirement to integrate SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) with  other third-party applications.

I would like to know:

  • Are there any standard APIs or integration methods provided by SAP IBP for such use cases?

  • Can we use standard OData, REST APIs, or CPI-based integration for connecting IBP with MRO systems?

  • Is there any recommended architecture or reference content available from SAP for such integrations?

Any guidance, documentation links, or examples of similar integrations would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance!
Best regards,

Satheesh Kumar.

entry.title: Re: PM Notifications in QMSM table
entry.content:
Hello jaya ,

Generally when you create a malfunction notification from plant maintenance module the table QMSM will store the malfunction notification data , tasks that are to be performed through that notifcation and catalog profile that is assigned to the notfication . This is one of the integration between Plant Maintenance and Quality Management .

Thanks & Regards ,
Neeraj.

entry.title: Re: Analytics Evolution : From Traditional Business Content to SAP BDC Intelligent Applications
entry.content:
Hello @harjisingh 

We wanted to look for more information on insight apps apart from the one that is currently available in our BDC cockpit. As of now, Only WCI (Working Capital Insights App) was available, and we are exploring it. My question is, where are the other apps? are they released? because we can't install them, and i can't find any relevant material about clearly stating the status. I think it may be authorization issues of my BDC cockpit that I can't download/explore them. 
So please share information related to that, and if they are not yet released, then when are they planned to?　and is there any information source you can provide, that i can read and gain more information, that would be very helpful. 

Thank you for your time and understanding!
ありがとうございます。

entry.title: How to develop a custom program to find 'Where Used List' of custom tables
entry.content:
I need to develop a custom program where there are more than 1000 custom tables for which I need a ‘where-used’ list. Using the standard ‘where-used’ list tool, I can check only one table at a time, which is very time-consuming. This is why I want to develop a program where we can provide multiple table names, and the program will generate the respective ‘where-used’ lists.

If you have any ideas on how I can achieve this, it would be very helpful.

entry.title: Re: External work centers to our operations in the work o...
entry.content:
Hello Jaya ,

We can assign the external work centers to the maintenance  order in operations tab by clicking on F4 help but there are some prerequisites :

1. The work center needs to be created against the work center .
2. The work center category needs to be 0005 - Plant Maintenance .

After creating work center you just need to create a maintenance order in that in operations tab you can find 

here the work center will appear and you need to select the appropriate work center here . And also if external services are needed that is to perform a task in operations tab through external service then we need to raise Purchase requestion and then the Material Managament people will convert it into Purchase order and then Service entry sheet .

Thanks & Regards ,
Neeraj.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

I'm a User Assistance Developer based out of India. I have provided user assistance for products like S/4 HANA, Concur, and Fieldglass.

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Hi!

I'm a User Assistance Developer based out of Bangalore, India. I have provided user assistance for products like S/4 HANA, Concur, and Fieldglass. I have grown in various cities of the country and studied at many different places. I call Mumbai -- the financial capital of India -- my home. I joined SAP about 3 years ago, after a 7-year-long career in academia. There hasn't been a dull moment in life since. 🙂

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud 是否有批次上傳【客戶訂金請求】及【結清收款-手動結清】的功能
entry.content:
Hi Fang,

For 1. APP【管理客戶訂金請求】(Manager Customer Down Payment Request) ,please refer to blog below

浅析API日记账分录-过账可以实现的场景-业务篇：应收账款（二）客户预付款申请

浅析API日记账分录-过账可以实现的场景-技术篇：应收账款(二) 客户预付款申请

For 2. APP【結清收款-手動結清】(Clear Incoming Payments-Manual Clearing)  ,please refer to API introduced in https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/5142cdd767b04122a8beb6ecd460a922.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud 是否有批次上傳【客戶訂金請求】及【結清收款-手動結清】的功能
entry.content:
Hi Fang,

For 1. APP【管理客戶訂金請求】(Manager Customer Down Payment Request) ,please refer to blog below

浅析API日记账分录-过账可以实现的场景-业务篇：应收账款（二）客户预付款申请

浅析API日记账分录-过账可以实现的场景-技术篇：应收账款(二) 客户预付款申请

For 2. APP【結清收款-手動結清】(Clear Incoming Payments-Manual Clearing)  ,please refer to API introduced in https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/5142cdd767b04122a8beb6ecd460a922.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud 是否有批次上傳【客戶訂金請求】及【結清收款-手動結清】的功能
entry.content:
Hi Fang,

1. APP【管理客戶訂金請求】(Manager Customer Down Payment Request)

Please refer to blogs below

浅析API日记账分录-过账可以实现的场景-技术篇：应收账款(二) 客户预付款申请

浅析API日记账分录-过账可以实现的场景-业务篇：应收账款（二）客户预付款申请
2. APP【結清收款-手動結清】(Clear Incoming Payments-Manual Clearing)

please refer to API introduced in help doc https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/5142cdd767b04122a8beb6ecd460a922.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: How to Schedule "Generate Source List" Fiori App as a...

Hello Jagoda,

Thank you for sharing the detailed information and helpful resources.

After reviewing the links, it appears that implementing a custom application job template will be the most suitable approach to meet our requirements. I truly appreciate your guidance and support.

Regards

Vinay

entry.title: SAP
entry.content: how to change the country postal code set in the sap public edition

entry.title: error in PREP_CHKIMPORT/INTCHK_IMP SUM phase
entry.content:
error in sum phase : PREP_CHKIMPORT/INTCHK_IMP 

Found 5745 entries in DDXTT. All entries are listed in log file CHECK_INACTNT_DDXTT.LOG.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Screen Enhancement PBO in SAP AS03 (Asset Master) - On-Premise 2020 vs. 2023
entry.content: I mean that it's not a problem of S/4HANA, not a problem of version, it's a problem at your side. As the screen is displayed, the PBO is executed. You say it's not executed but it's impossible that it's not executed. Probably your definition of "MODULE modulename OUTPUT" contains a typo: missing word "OUTPUT" for instance, or typo in the module name which doesn't correspond to the name in the screen flow logic "MODULE modulename"..

entry.title: Dunning F150 - separate letter for the payer
entry.content:
Hello,

I have cases when on one customer's account there are invoices to be paid either by sold-to-party or different payer. Is there a possibility to configure system so that a separate letter is generated to sold-to-party and separate one for the payer? 

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Unlocking the Future: How SAP Build Stands Out in Application Development
entry.content:
SAP Build stands out in the application development market due to its unique combination of low-code/no-code capabilities, deep integration with SAP ecosystems, AI-driven features, and focus on enterprise-grade scalability and governance. Below is a detailed analysis of why SAP Build differentiates itself from competitors like OutSystems, Mendix, or Microsoft Power Apps, emphasizing its strengths in the context of digital transformation and application development.

1. Unified Low-Code/No-Code Platform

  • Comprehensive Suite: SAP Build integrates three core components—SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Work Zone—into a single platform, enabling end-to-end development of applications, process automation, and business sites. Unlike competitors that often focus on one aspect (e.g., app development or automation), SAP Build provides a holistic solution, reducing the need for multiple tools.
  • Citizen Developer Empowerment: Its intuitive drag-and-drop interface and prebuilt templates allow business users with minimal technical expertise to create enterprise-grade applications. This democratizes development, enabling faster prototyping and reducing dependency on specialized developers, which competitors like OutSystems also offer but with less seamless SAP integration.

Standout Factor: The unified platform lowers the learning curve and streamlines workflows, making it ideal for businesses seeking rapid development without fragmented toolsets.

2. Deep Integration with SAP Ecosystem

  • SAP BTP Foundation: Built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), SAP Build seamlessly integrates with SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, and other SAP solutions. This ensures a single source of truth, real-time data access, and compatibility with SAP’s clean core strategy, which minimizes technical debt during upgrades.
  • Extensibility: The Extensibility Wizard (introduced at SAP TechEd 2024) simplifies customization for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and is planned for SAP SuccessFactors, enabling tailored solutions without complex coding. Competitors like Mendix or Salesforce Lightning require additional integration layers to achieve similar SAP connectivity.
  • Third-Party Integration: SAP Build supports integration with non-SAP systems (e.g., Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics) via SAP Integration Suite, with event-driven architecture planned for Q4 2025, enhancing real-time data flows.

Standout Factor: Native integration with SAP systems and support for hybrid environments make SAP Build the preferred choice for organizations heavily invested in SAP, unlike general-purpose platforms that require custom connectors.

3. AI-Driven Innovation

  • Joule Studio: SAP Build leverages AI through Joule Studio, enabling the creation of AI agents for predictive analytics, process automation, and intelligent decision-making. For example, it can automate complex workflows like invoice matching or predict inventory needs, a feature less prevalent in platforms like Appian or ServiceNow.
  • Embedded Intelligence: Integration with SAP AI Core allows developers to embed machine learning models into applications, enhancing functionality like demand forecasting or anomaly detection without external tools.
  • Natural Language Processing: Joule’s conversational AI simplifies user interactions, making app development and process automation accessible to non-technical users.

Standout Factor: AI-driven capabilities, tightly integrated with SAP’s enterprise data, provide a competitive edge over platforms with generic AI tools, delivering context-aware solutions for business-specific challenges.

4. Enterprise-Grade Scalability and Governance

  • Scalability: Built on SAP BTP’s cloud infrastructure, SAP Build supports enterprise-scale deployments with high availability and performance. It handles large-scale transformations, such as SAP S/4HANA migrations, with minimal latency, unlike smaller platforms like Bubble, which are better suited for startups.
  • Governance and Compliance: SAP Build includes built-in governance frameworks ensuring GDPR, SOC 2, and other compliance standards, critical for industries like finance or healthcare. This contrasts with platforms like Zoho Creator, which may require additional configurations for enterprise compliance.
  • Clean Core Strategy: SAP Build aligns with RISE with SAP’s clean core principles, ensuring extensions are upgradable and maintainable, reducing long-term costs compared to platforms like OutSystems, where customizations can lead to technical debt.

Standout Factor: Robust governance and scalability make SAP Build a trusted choice for large enterprises with complex regulatory and scalability needs.

5. Rapid Time-to-Value

  • Accelerated Development: SAP Build’s low-code approach reduces development time by 50-70% compared to traditional coding, as seen in case studies like Open Grid Europe, which achieved 50% faster onboarding using SAP Build Apps. Prebuilt templates and reusable components further speed up deployment.
  • Process Automation: SAP Build Process Automation combines RPA and workflow management, reducing manual tasks by up to 80% (e.g., financial close processes). This is faster than competitors like UiPath, which focus primarily on RPA without integrated app development.
  • Guided Methodologies: Integration with SAP Activate and SAP Signavio ensures structured implementation, minimizing project risks and accelerating time-to-value.

Standout Factor: The combination of low-code development, automation, and guided methodologies delivers faster ROI, particularly for SAP-centric transformations, compared to platforms with longer setup times.

6. Enhanced Collaboration and User Experience

  • Business-IT Collaboration: SAP Build bridges the gap between business and IT teams through shared development environments and visual tools, reducing miscommunication. For example, business users can prototype apps, while IT ensures governance, unlike Mendix, where IT involvement is often heavier.
  • SAP Build Work Zone: Provides role-based, personalized portals with SAP Fiori integration, improving user adoption by 25-40% (SAP benchmarks). This contrasts with platforms like Microsoft Power Apps, which offer less tailored UX for SAP environments.
  • Community and Support: SAP’s global partner ecosystem (e.g., EY, AG) and SAP Community provide extensive training and support, ensuring successful adoption compared to smaller platforms with limited ecosystems.

Standout Factor: Enhanced collaboration and user-centric design drive higher adoption rates, making SAP Build ideal for organizations prioritizing user engagement.

7. Competitive Positioning

Compared to key competitors:

  • OutSystems: Strong in low-code but lacks deep SAP integration and clean core alignment.
  • Mendix: Offers robust low-code features but requires more effort for SAP connectivity and compliance.
  • Microsoft Power Apps: Excels in Microsoft ecosystems but is less optimized for SAP environments.
  • ServiceNow: Focuses on IT service management, with limited app development flexibility compared to SAP Build’s comprehensive suite.

SAP Build’s tight integration with SAP BTP, AI capabilities, and focus on enterprise needs give it an edge for organizations leveraging SAP systems or pursuing large-scale digital transformations.

8. Real-World Impact

  • Open Grid Europe: Extended SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build, achieving 50% faster onboarding and 20% reduced invoicing time.
  • Multinational Manufacturer: Used SAP Build with EY to standardize processes, improving efficiency across functions within 12 months.
  • Utilities Sector: Leveraged SAP Build for metering and billing apps, reducing process steps and enhancing real-time visibility.

Conclusion

SAP Build stands out in the application development market due to its unified low-code/no-code platform, deep SAP integration, AI-driven innovation, and enterprise-grade scalability. Its ability to accelerate development, automate processes, and ensure compliance while fostering collaboration makes it a leader for organizations undergoing SAP-centric or hybrid digital transformations. For businesses invested in SAP ecosystems or seeking rapid, scalable solutions, SAP Build delivers unmatched value, positioning it as a top choice in the competitive low-code market.

SAP Build  SAP AI Services SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

entry.title: Re: Problem in BDC recording for Transaction code - OVXC
entry.content:
Hi Bhavin,

After trying this in my system, it appears that this is a limitation of SHDB, as it fails to record the check box selection in the popup screen. You might consider manually adding the screen and field information into your recording program and giving it a try (though this is not certain).

If that approach doesn't work for your scenario, another option is to look into table TVSWZ, which stores the Shipping Point and Plant assignment data.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Can't instantiate a fragment after Destory it
entry.content: I'm destroying the view because it became useless 99% of the time. I'm not destroying the fragment because, reading the docs, I understood that 'destroy view implies destroy fragment.'

entry.title: Re: What product do you register HANA Cockpit as on the S...
entry.content:
Hello,

You can try "SAP HANA, platform edition". SAP HANA cockpit is under the scope of SAP HANA Platform, where you can find the appropriate path and component to report a case to SAP Support.

br,
Rainie

entry.title: Payment schedule
entry.content:
Payment schedule.

First step - A purchase order was created, with an account assignment category for the project (WBS element) and mandatory down payments on the items of the purchase order.

The initial data is as follows:

Purchase cost – 90,000.00

Expected delivery date 01.10.2025

Expected payment date of the Advance payment 60% - 01.06.2025

Expected post-payment date in 90 days from the delivery date – 30.12.2025

 

Accordingly, the expected financing (liability) for the order for 90,000.00 by period looks as follows:

01.06.2025 – 54,000.00

30.12.2025 – 36,000.00

In the CJIA report, the financial liability (type 51) for the above order is displayed in the 1st line with the expected payment date as 30.12.2025 – 90,000.00, which does not comply with the terms of the contract and does not take into account the need to pay an advance of 54,000.00 on 01.06.2025, as well as the payment amount on 31.12.2025 is incorrectly formed (correctly 36,000.00).

 

How to ensure the reflection of the expected financing in terms of periods, taking into account advance payments?

entry.title: Re: How to develop a custom program to find 'Where Used List' of custom tables
entry.content: Use the SQL trace and the ABAP trace to see how the standard works. There are probably several answers in the forum how it works, which may be difficult to find, so search something like RS_EU_CROSSREF, WBCROSSGT. Note that they may work or not depending on your ABAP version.

entry.title: Re: Can't instantiate a fragment after Destory it
entry.content: What about destryoing the view? I need to do it because in my webapp there are a lot of views that can be used and I need to destroy them if no longer usefull; users can still use them but that could be very rare. The fragment issue occurs when i destroy the view and then i re-istanziate it

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Trial Account Creation and Enabling Integration Suite service(SAP CPI)
entry.content:
APIManagement.Selfservice.Administrator role collection is needed to work on the API exercise.

After assigning the role collection, you need to logout and login in Integration Suite page. And click Setting --> API. There is a dialog box telling you that the API is in provision. Then take some time and logout/login again, you will see the completed Menu as in the exercise.

entry.title: Is there any API that can list all versions of an Artifact?
entry.content:
When using APIs within 'Integration Content', sometimes should pass 'Version'.

However, there are chances that I don't know versions of a specific Artifact(Integration Flow, Script Collection, etc...), or I don't want to get the latest version, but a previous version:

For example, I want to list all versions of a Artifact, like here ['5.2.0', '1.0.8', ...], then I choose to send a GET request for version '1.0.0'.

Is it possible that I can get all existing Versions of an Artifact?

entry.title: Re: Data Initialization Issue in CAP Java Project with HA...
entry.content:
Hi @stefaniaZ,

I just saw this recent blog about data loss when deploying from CAP to HANA .

The problem looks similar, I hope this helps.

entry.title: Need to Get a Comment field in Workflow when HR Tries to Approve/Decline leaves.
entry.content:
Hi All,

The required flow is:

When the employee applies a leave it should flow to HR group.
The requirement is to get or add a mandatory remarks option for HR and it is a one level approval mechanism.
When the HR approves the leave, the mail notification in cc should flow to IRM/HR Team and whatever the comment updated by HR has to be visible or trigger in cc mail notification only to HR Team not the manager.

Able to configure all the mechanism except getting the comment field in workflow and capturing the mandatory comment flowing to HR Team in mail part. Whatever the comments added by HR need to be captured/Updated  in the triggered mail(mail received by the HR-Team).

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Data Initialization Issue in CAP Java Project with HA...
entry.content:
Hi valentincadart,

thanks so much.

 

Stefania

entry.title: Two OPEN SQl statements with same WHERE condition, different Columns but returning different numbers
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need your help with the OPEN SQL statements shared below. I'm comparing two SQL statements that use the same WHERE condition but have different numbers of columns. I expect both statements to return the same number of records since the WHERE condition is the same. However, when I execute the SQL statements, I find that the number of records returned is different. Can someone explain why this is happening?

SELECT plpo~plnty,
           plpo~plnnr,
           plpo~plnkn,
           plpo~zaehl,
           plpo~vgw01,
           plpo~vgw02,
           plpo~vgw03,
           plpo~bmsch,
           plpo~vornr,
           plpo~vge01,
           plpo~vge02,
           plpo~vge03,
           plpo~steus,
           plas~loekz,
           plas~plnfl,
           plas~plnal,
           crhd~arbpl,
           crhd~objid
       FROM plpo
          INNER JOIN plas ON plpo~plnty = plas~plnty
           AND plpo~plnnr = plas~plnnr
           AND plpo~plnkn = plas~plnkn
           AND plpo~zaehl = plas~zaehl
         INNER JOIN crhd ON plpo~arbid = crhd~objid
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @t_operazioni
         WHERE plpo~plnty = @t_operazioni-plnty
           AND plpo~plnnr = @t_operazioni-plnnr
           AND plas~plnal = @t_operazioni-plnal
           AND plpo~steus <> 'ZP00'
           AND plas~loekz IS INITIAL
           AND plas~plnfl = '000000'
      INTO TABLE @DATA(t_plpo).


    SELECT
           plpo~vgw01,
           plpo~vgw02,
           plpo~vgw03,
           plpo~bmsch,
           plpo~vornr,
           plpo~vge01,
           plpo~vge02,
           plpo~vge03,
           crhd~arbpl

            FROM plpo
           INNER JOIN plas ON plpo~plnty = plas~plnty
                         AND plpo~plnnr = plas~plnnr
                         AND plpo~plnkn = plas~plnkn
                         AND plpo~zaehl = plas~zaehl
          INNER  JOIN crhd ON plpo~arbid = crhd~objid
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @t_operazioni
            WHERE plpo~plnty = @t_operazioni-plnty AND
                plpo~plnnr = @t_operazioni-plnnr AND
                plas~plnal = @t_operazioni-plnal AND
                plpo~steus <> 'ZP00' AND
                plas~loekz IS INITIAL AND
                plas~plnfl = '000000'
    INTO TABLE @DATA(t_plpo2).

entry.title: Re: ABAP code or scripting in CI-DS where source and target both are IBP
entry.content:
Hi April,

So if I have to achieve complex logic in IBP source-target scenario, I have to rely only on the transforms and the functions? I know that there are options to write post and pre-load scripts. Is there anything similar that will be called at each iteration of records, instead of only at the beginning and end?

Regards,

Aparna B

entry.content: any solution?

entry.title: Re: Create 2 bits from a cit but to different business pa...
entry.content:
Hi Eric,

A follow-up question on this. Since the contract is created with a particular Partner and CA, will there be any issue in CI after the BIT has been generated? Since the partner and Contract Account do not belong to the provider contract, will there be any issues with BIT creation, Billing, or Invoicing?

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: What’s New as of 2025-06-09?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community members!

Here again to give you the latest updates in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud – Standard Model. From Modeling screen, Administration screens such as: Teams and Settings, and Show Screen (Pivot Mode = On).

Without further ado, Let’s dive into What’s New in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model.

 

Modeling

1. New mapping fields are now available for RFA FI-GL and RFA FI-GLI

As of today, Remote Function Adapter types, Finance General Ledger and Finance General Ledger Items, now have both Financial Period and Tax Code fields which modelers can now use when posting documents.

Finance General Ledger

header-fis_periodFiscal Period
accountgl[ 1 ]-tax_codeTax on Sales/Purchases Code
accountgl[ 2 ]-tax_codeTax on Sales/Purchases Code

Finance General Ledger Items

header-fis_periodFiscal Period
accountgl-tax_codeTax on Sales/Purchases Code

2. Modeling Update (Join)

For SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model modelers, get ready to experience the environment modeling like never before! We're incredibly excited to unveil our latest upgrade, packed with innovative features designed to provide better user experience and satisfaction! For full details about this update, visit SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: Modeling Update (Join)

 

Show Screen (Pivot Mode = On)

As we know, when the pivot mode is set to "On" in the Show screen, the can show the totals for a particular column.

As an addition to this feature, users will now be able to customize the "Result" label for totals by following the simple steps below.

  1. Choose the column header option
  2. Choose Change Aggregation Label and adjust the label text
  3. and then choose Confirm

 

Teams

As part of the continuous enhancement to have flexibility in team management, the team collection which can contain child teams is now possible in the application. 

For the configuration and more info, you may refer to SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model – Introducing Team Collection blogpost.

 

Settings Screen

1. Email Notifications Update

To align with SAP's email handling capabilities, we are now transitioning from the current SAP SMTP service to a new solution, ANS (Alert Notification Service). For the full details, visit this blogpost SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: 3 Things You Need to Know About the Update in Email Notifications

This update also includes user interface improvements which will provide admin with the ability to choose the configured destination from BTP in the Settings screen.

2. For role collection ADMIN_USER_RESTRICTED, we introduced a small change in its restriction to provide more secure granularity in which this role collection will now not have the ability to perform any password activities such as reset or unlock of the current database user password.

ADMIN_USER_RESTRICTED

This change does not affect other role collections such as:

SETTINGS and ADMIN_USER_ALL

The SETTINGS_READ role will remain the same with read-only access to the Administration Settings screen.

3. SAP Cloud ALM Integration in PaPM Cloud Standard Model

We're excited to announce the integration of SAP Profitability and Performance Management (PaPM) Cloud Standard Model with SAP Cloud ALM. This integration allows you to monitor the health of your PaPM application in one place.

To use this integration, you need to be subscribed to SAP PaPM Cloud and SAP Cloud ALM. The integration focuses on health monitoring, and you can track key aspects like:

  • Connection Monitoring: Checks if PaPM can connect to the database.
  • Availability Monitoring: Confirms the database is running and logs any errors.

In the Administration Settings screen, you can define which destination, previously configured for SAP Cloud ALM in SAP BTP, will be used in PaPM. This new option is available in the "Destination Settings" section. You can choose the destination from a dropdown menu filled with pre-configured SAP BTP destinations. The field allows for quick search. As you type, a dropdown shows filtered results, making it simple to find the desired destination

If the destination name is not specified on the Settings page, the system will automatically check for the existence of the default destination name,  "PaPM_CloudALM," in SAP BTP. The system will also check for the default destination if the user attempts to save an empty destination field. If the default destination is created in SAP BTP, it will be populated in the field. If it is not defined, the field will remain empty.

We have added a field under the Application Shell Integration Settings section where you can add your SAP Cloud ALM link.

When you add a link for SAP Cloud ALM under the Application Shell Integration Settings section, a new tile will be activated in the app switcher on the Shell Page, from which the user can access the SAP Cloud ALM application.

For more information, visit  Settings in the Application Help Portal.  

For more information on integrating SAP Cloud ALM with SAP Profitability and Performance Management and creating a destination for it in SAP BTP, visit  SAP Cloud ALM Setup in the Administration Guide.

That is all! If you want to monitor and recall what are the new features and updates from SAP PaPM Cloud - Standard Model, you may follow the tag PaPMCloudSMWhatsNew thank you for reading!

entry.title: SAP-консультант: хто це та як ним стати
entry.content:
💼SAP-консультант — перспективна професія на стику бізнесу й технологій.

Хто це, які бувають ролі, як увійти в професію та з чого почати - дізнавайтеся на вебінарі від LABA

📅  12 червня, 19:00 за Києвом

👤 Лектор Іван Хряпін, SAP-фінансовий консультант і проєктний менеджер у CRH

Для кого: світчери, SAP-консультанти-початківці, IT-фахівці та бізнес-аналітики

📌 Реєструйтесь за посиланням!

entry.title: Unable to pull the data correctly from LMS report.
entry.content:
Hi,

We have created one LMS custom report in SuccessFactors (Item Completion and Incompletion Report), we are able to pull the report regarding DEBS legal entity(Latest) people but not able to pull regarding DARH Legal Entity(Old) employees from Domain's filter. Also we are able to pull the user's Completion & incompletion courses data from Items filter but not from Domain's filter from user's filter.

FYI: DARH users are already having Past profiles nd now moved from DKSA to DARH and in report we are getting Past assignment's user ID while pulling the report and submitting the criteria from item filter.

Could you please help me on this, that what could be the issue.

Thanks & Best Regards,

Sri Ramya.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content:
Dear @igorportu 

thanks for using our offering!

The passwords are listed always in the README - section "user and passwords" - located here:

https://hub.docker.com/r/sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial#user-and-passwords 

Kind regards,
Stephan
SAP - ABAP Cloud Developer Trial Team

entry.title: Re: OdataV2 based API for creating Billing Document
entry.content: Hi, Sudarshan005. I also have the same problem as you. I would like know if you found a way to create billing docs via Odata API. Thanks.

entry.title: Timesheet Module Behavior on Attendance and Leave Collision
entry.content:
Hello Experts,


I’m seeking your guidance regarding a standard behavior observed in the Timesheet module, specifically related to entry blocking when a collision occurs on a single day.

Issue Summary:
When both attendance and leave type (PL, CL,SL, Out Door Duty) or any absences are recorded on the same day, the system flags a collision error. As a result, it blocks timesheet entries for the remaining days of that week. However, once the collision is resolved, the timesheet updates correctly for the entire week.

We understand that this is standard system behavior.

Request for Suggestions:
Is there a way to prevent the system from blocking the remaining days of the week when such a conflict occurs on just one day?

If anyone has implemented a workaround or alternative solution to handle this scenario more gracefully, we would greatly appreciate your insights or suggestions.

entry.title: Custom Field Extension in Quality Notification with Domain-Based Value Help
entry.content:
Dear Guru,

I tried to extend the Quality Notification using the Custom Fields app by adding a new field.
The business requirement is that this custom field should be linked to a standard table using a domain with a value table, similar to how the PS_POSNR domain works.

I noticed that the Custom Fields app allows creating a custom field for a standard business object, and after publishing, the corresponding table shows the Z-field with the assigned standard domain and its associated value table.

Is it possible to simulate this behavior intentionally, ensuring the field uses a domain with a value table, and that it functions the same way?

Release : Sap on premise 2023

Best regards,

Laura

entry.title: Re: Facing issue in SAP CPI trial version
entry.content: I am also facing same issue science Friday. Do you got any solution.

entry.title: Re: Carry Over Leave Balance to Next Year Until Specific Date Automatically using Integration Center

entry.title: Re: Fetching the Count from OData service response
entry.content: I realized, it might be something very easy reply to this. I found out the solution to it. Instead of exploring the formula option, the correct approach was to bind from the Output value of the previous node (Get Records Collection) node:

entry.title: Re: PM Notifications in QMSM table
entry.content:
Hi Neeraj,

Thank you for the response!

Can you please let me know why some notifications are not appearing in QMSM table. For these notifications if we go and check QMEL table we find that the field MAKNZ is blank.

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Re: Verification field on RF screen is not editable
entry.content:
Hi Daniil,

Sorry for late response.  We will use Product field as it is accepting barcode. 

Thank you for your quick help.

Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: Re: Two OPEN SQl statements with same WHERE condition, di...
entry.content:
@bamjimmy 

Hi!

You have a "FOR ALL ENTRIES" construct in your SELECT statement and this has the effect that it only gives DISTINCT values back in the results. I don't know the tables involved in your statement but you may need to add whatever the key fields for PLPO are in the field list of your 2nd select to ensure that you get all the expected entries (even if you don't necessarily need the fields in the subsequent logic).

Hope this helps!

Thank you for the response!

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Error when upload LTMC PIR with condition in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Expert, 

Here, I meet an issue while uploading PIR, especially in the condition tab. When I create PIR manually, the mandatory price is defined as net price and set by the system as PPR0. 

But when I upload LTMC PIR and input PPR0, this error appears

 

So I added PNP0 as the net price. Then this issue appears

Why I have to maintain PNP0 in my custom pricing procedure? However, when I create PIR manually, I don't have to input PNP0.

Please give me advice related to this issue 

MM Purchasing

entry.title: Re: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end - Event-driven-architecture - Event framework
entry.content:
Hi @AshishPandey 

There is no "program to run", I'm not even sur what you mean by that. My scenario is end-to-end and all steps for local consumption are included. Please refer to the troubleshooting part of my blog. BGPF needs to be setup properly. If you only can debug via SBGRFCMON then that tells me BGPF is probably not fully setup. Check with your basis. If this doesn't solve the issue, I recommend creating a proper ticket because this is not something that can be helped via text only and needs to be checked in your system.

entry.title: Re: How to Connect Eclipse with Git or abapGit and Push A...
entry.content: Hi @SabineReich, Thanks for sharing the tutorial.

entry.title: Re: How to Connect Eclipse with Git or abapGit and Push A...
entry.content: Hi @mainak-aich, Thanks for sharing the detailed steps.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Screen Enhancement PBO in SAP AS03 (Asset Master) - On-Premise 2020 vs. 2023
entry.content: There is no issue in the naming and syntax,as our team tried the same scenarios in different systems.

entry.title: Re: Facing issue in SAP CPI trial version
entry.content: Hi here also I am facing same issue in trail account

entry.title: How to access and modify the data in QM01 from user-exit QQMA0001?
entry.content:
Hello,

In transaction QM01 when I create a notification type 'Q3' I want to display a new subscreen with Purchase Doc. and Item similar as 'Q2' type notification. In order to do it I'm trying to do it with user exit QQMA0001 and I'm able to create a new subscreen with these fields but I also want to be get the material and plant from the Purchase Doc/Item which is entered and set it to the next subscreen ( standard ) and I'm not being able to achieve it since the PBO of this standard subscreen is not triggered. I already tried to fill the E_VIQMEL but it's being cleaned and not propagated to this field...

 

Any ideas on how to do it?

entry.title: Add Custom Fields in S/4HANA Public Cloud Migration Cockpit Template for Product Master
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,


We are in the final stages of our S/4HANA Public Cloud go-live and are using the Migration Cockpit (Migrate Your Data App) for product master data migration.


We need to include the following custom fields in the Product master - Source Data template as they are critical for our operations and data integrity:




  2. Document




  4. Basic Data Text




  6. Document Version




  8. Inspection Text




  10. Internal Comment



These fields are PERSON_NAME from a business perspective and essential for completing the product data upload before go-live.


Kindly guide us on the recommended approach:




  • How to enhance the migration template in S/4HANA Public Cloud?




  • Can we use the "Custom Migration Object" app or key user extensibility to achieve this?




  • If not feasible directly, what is the SAP-recommended alternative for loading these fields?



Please treat this as Go-Live Critical and advise urgently.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Add Custom Fields in S/4HANA Public Cloud Migration C...
entry.content:
To add the required Custom fields you can use the app Migration Object Modeler.

Please refer to the below blog for the required steps

How to work with Standard Migration Object in Migration Object Modeler App

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
This comment will be the place holder for SAP Enterprise Architecture subjects in the context of SAP Modernisation, Transformation, S/4HANA, PCE, RISE, Cloud, BTP....

Discovering SAP Enterprise Architecture

What's New in SAP Reference Architecture? [Spring ... - SAP Community thanks to @JuliaDorotheeMeyer 

Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What's New in Q2 2... - SAP Community thanks to @chriskollhed 

What's New in SAP Reference Architecture? [Spring ... - SAP Community

SAP Discovery Center Architecture - Establish a central entry point with SAP Build Work Zone

Data & Analytics | SAP Architecture Center

entry.title: Re: Issue with Screen Enhancement PBO in SAP AS03 (Asset Master) - On-Premise 2020 vs. 2023
entry.content: That's probably something not obvious, but that only your team can detect, as people here can't log into your system to check the issue. You may contact the SAP Support, open your system, and they will check.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Scheduling Board in Fiori - Alternative Reso...
entry.content:
Dear Thomas,

 

I imagine your issue has been solved as the question was from 2 years ago, however I found your question before I found the solution on SAP support page so I wanted to provide it here as well for other people struggling with the same. What you described is an SAP bug and they have fixed it with SAP note 3501967 https://me.sap.com/notes/3501967

All the best,

 

Radu
